draft.introduction: A psalm of divine protection promising safety under God’s wings. The devil quotes verses 11–12 during the temptation of Christ, making it central to NT interpretation.

translation.text: He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High will abide in the shadow of the Almighty. I will say to the Lord, “My refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.” You will not fear the terror of the night, nor the arrow that flies by day.
translation.original_text: ὁ κατοικῶν ἐν βοηθείᾳ τοῦ ὑψίστου

translation.text: Because you have made the Lord your dwelling place, no evil shall befall you. For he will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways. On their hands they will bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.
translation.original_text: ὅτι σύ κύριε ἡ ἐλπίς μου τὸν ὕψιστον ἔθου καταφυγήν σου

translator_notes.note: These verses are quoted by Satan in the temptation narrative (Matt 4:6, Luke 4:10-11). The use of Scripture by the adversary became a patristic proof-text for the danger of reading Scripture without proper interpretive tradition.
